intro: The most concrete split between rrweb and Zipy is the pricing model: rrweb runs on free and open-source, while Zipy uses subscription from $0/month, with a free tier. That shapes how predictable your monthly spend is and how much of the cost sits in infrastructure versus per-seat or per-event fees. Beyond the headline, rrweb carries low lock-in (oss session replay lib) and free and open-source; Zipy carries medium lock-in (session replay data exportable) and subscription from $0/month, with a free tier. rrweb is a fit when you are engineers building custom replay, qa or debugging tooling in-house. Zipy is a fit when you are ux debugging and optimization. The honest trade-off: rrweb is explicitly not for non-technical teams expecting a hosted dashboard out of the box; Zipy is explicitly not for apps with strict privacy requirements (healthcare). On the product side, rrweb is described as Open-source web session replay library.
quick_take: rrweb is for engineers building custom replay, qa or debugging tooling in-house; Zipy is for ux debugging and optimization; decide on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose rrweb if you want a fully free, open-source option and can handle self-hosting or self-support.
verdict_b: Choose Zipy if its subscription pricing model fits your budget shape better than rrweb's free model, and you are ux debugging and optimization.

faq.a: Direct comparison is hard. rrweb is free and open-source, so licence cost is zero though you pay in operational effort. Zipy uses subscription from $0/month, with a free tier, so it costs money in exchange for managed service. Which is cheaper depends on how much engineering time you value.

faq.q: Can I migrate from rrweb to Zipy?
faq.a: Migration from rrweb to Zipy is plausible because rrweb has low lock-in (oss session replay lib). You would still have to map config, permissions and integrations to Zipy's model. Plan for dual-run and rollback.

On our scoring rrweb rates 5/5 for developer experience versus 4/5 for Zipy. That suggests day-to-day workflows (CLI, SDKs, docs) are smoother in rrweb. Scores are directional; run a short spike on your own stack to confirm.

faq.q: Is Zipy a good alternative to rrweb?
faq.a: Zipy is pitched at ux debugging and optimization, which overlaps with rrweb's fit (engineers building custom replay, qa or debugging tooling in-house) but is not identical. Note that Zipy is explicitly not for apps with strict privacy requirements (healthcare), so it only replaces rrweb if you are outside that exclusion. The lock-in profile differs (low vs medium), which is the main thing to weigh.
